DISCOVERY 301, HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION IS GRANTED AS TO 75 PERCENT OF THE ACCOUNT BALANCE. THE REMAINING 25 PERCENT SHALL BE PAID TO THE JUDGMENT CREDITOR. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH; NOT REPORTED
EXECUTION FOR MONEY ISSUED TO SACRAMENTO COUNTY AS TO DEFENDANT WONG, JOSEPHINE C.

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF CACH, LLC'S HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION IS DENIED. HALF OF THE MONEY HELD, $507.45 IS TO BE RELEASED TO THE JUDGMENT CREDITOR AND THE OTHER HALF, $507.46, IS TO BE RELEASED TO THE JUDGMENT DEBTOR. THE JUDGMENT DEBTOR HAS NOT PROVIDED ANY EVIDENCE TRACING THE FUNDS TO AN EXEMPT SOURCE. HOWEVER, JUDGMENT DEBTOR'S FINANCIAL STATEMENT SHOWS THAT AT LEAST SOME OF THE MONEY IS NEEDED TO MEET HER BASIC LIVING NEEDS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD CSR #7077

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption
Off Calendar Mar-27-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - JOSEPHINE C. WONG, TEL. NO. 308-1186, DEFENDANT APPEARING IN PRO PER;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF CACH, LLC'S Hearing On Claim Of Exemption is Denied. Half of the money held, $507.45 is to be released to the judgment creditor and the other half, $507.46, is to be released to the judgment debtor. The judgment debtor has not provided any evidence tracing the funds to an exempt source. However, judgment debtor's financial statement shows that at least some of the money is needed to meet her basic living needs. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077 =(302/HEK)

Ntc Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption
Off Calendar Oct-19-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: Hearing On Claim Of Exemption is granted as to 75 percent of the account balance. The remaining 25 percent shall be paid to the Judgment Creditor. Judge: Peter J. Busch; Not reported = (301/PJB)
